plot_ratio: 1.0-2.0 town centre/brownfield; 0.5-1.0 inner suburban; 0.35-0.5 / 0.25-0.35 outer suburban
separation_distance_m: 22 m between opposing above-ground-floor windows; 11 m min rear garden depth
private_open_space: per-bedroom minimums in table (e.g. 48 m² / 85 m² / 6 m²)
public_open_space_pct: 15% of total site area (minimum); <500 sqm not taken in charge
car_parking_dwelling: House (<=3 bed): 1 space + 0.5 visitor for 4+ bed; Apartment: 1.5 per unit + 1 visitor per 4
trigger_AA: Screening for AA for all plans/projects unless directly connected to Natura 2000 management; full AA/NIS where indicated (15.1.6)
trigger_EIA: EIA Directive (2011/92/EU as amended); significant effects on environment (15.1.5)
trigger_FRA: SuDS/surface water management (15.8.1); flood risk via SFRA + sequential approach (no separate Justification Test trigger in chapter)
trigger_AHIA: Development affecting protected structures / ACAs per Architectural Heritage Protection Guidelines (2011)
trigger_TTA: TTA for larger developments per Table 15.7; trigger e.g. traffic exceeds 10% of adjoining road flow (TII 2014)
notes: Density cross-referenced to Table 3.1 of Chapter 3, not fixed in Ch.15. Rural housing per Kildare Rural House Design Guide (Appendix 4); no fixed min site area. Social infrastructure audit for >20 units or >2,000 m². Justification Test/percolation not enumerated verbatim. Setbacks measured from nearest edge of road surface.
